title: Determine the exact value of R(B16, B16)
micro_paper_assessment: Unsolved, but the solve run produced a real theorem slice: any 65-vertex witness would have to be strongly regular with parameters (65,32,15,16). The package is still short of the micro-paper lane until the existence question for that graph is settled.
family_affinity: high; the reduction naturally extends to the diagonal one-gap family on 4n+1 vertices
generalization_signal: strong; any 4n+1-vertex two-color Bn witness would have to be srg(4n+1,2n,n-1,n)
candidate_theorem_slice: Any graph on 65 vertices with no B16 in either the graph or its complement is strongly regular with parameters (65,32,15,16).
next_action: Verify the Goodman-saturation reduction and then check whether srg(65,32,15,16) or an equivalent symmetric conference matrix of order 66 is already known; if not, attack the 65-vertex existence question using the published 64-vertex template family under the forced 32/15/16 local constraints.
